description: §6.6.9 (RULING: THE SPLIT) — a §52 `<... server>` cell read in a WHOLLY-server fn is E-REACTIVE-003, same as any client cell. Correction to the earlier draft: a `<price server>` cell is CLIENT-HELD (compiler-hydrated on mount, §52.4.2; the persist is the developer's own `?{}` fn, §52.6.2) — there is NO server-side cell store, so a server-side read lowers to `_scrml_body["price"]` and resolves to `undefined`. `bump()` has no reactive assignment (not CPS), so the read is unmarshalled → error. Incidental W-AUTH-001 ignored by superset.
rationale: §6.6.9: a §52 `<... server>` cell is client-held; a wholly-server read is not transported and resolves to undefined, so it fires E-REACTIVE-003 identically to a plain client cell. (The earlier 'server-resolved, exempt' premise was empirically false.)
